title: SZL Governed Inference Receipt
description: Schema for a single SZL runtime governance decision receipt (the decoded decision object). A receipt is the small, replayable, hash-chained record that a governed runtime emits for one governed action (e.g. an inference). It is NOT zero-knowledge and NOT a cryptographic proof of computation; it is a signed, verifiable audit record of what the runtime decided. Field set is grounded in the real receipts published in SZLHOLDINGS/a11oy-verifiable-corpus (lake/ and receipts/), SZLHOLDINGS/readiness-runs, and SZLHOLDINGS/szl-evidence. Fields not present in current real receipts are marked optional and documented as spec extensions; measured values (e.g. energy joules) are never fabricated and are null when unmeasured.

action.description: The governed action this receipt attests. Real value seen: 'inference'.

ns.description: Namespace / product surface that emitted the receipt (e.g. 'a11oy').

organ.description: Optional sub-surface / routed organ within the namespace (real value seen: 'vertical-general').

seq.description: Monotonic sequence number within a hash chain. The genesis receipt has seq 0 and prev = 64 zero hex chars.

prev.description: SHA-256 (64 lowercase hex) of the previous receipt's `digest`, forming a hash chain. The genesis receipt uses 64 zero characters.

digest.description: Chain-link digest for this receipt. The next receipt's `prev` MUST equal this value. Computed by the emitting runtime over the canonical receipt body; the offline verifier checks the CHAIN relation (prev == previous.digest) and the DSSE PAE content hash rather than re-deriving this internal digest.

payload_digest.description: SHA-256 (64 lowercase hex) of the underlying governed payload (e.g. the inference request/response content). The content itself is intentionally NOT embedded in the receipt; the digest binds to it.

ts.description: Emission timestamp. Real receipts use a Unix epoch seconds float; ISO-8601 UTC strings are also accepted.

decision.description: The governance verdict for this action. Real value seen: 'allow'. 'deny'/'block' express the deny-by-default 'honest-blocked' posture (see szl-blocked). 'review'/'abstain' are permitted for held/undecided actions.

honest_blocked.description: OPTIONAL spec flag for the deny-by-default 'honest-blocked' semantics (szl-blocked): true when the runtime withheld/blocked the action and is honest about it. In current real receipts the verdict is carried by `decision`; this explicit flag is a spec extension and MAY be absent.

lambda.description: OPTIONAL Λ governance-floor score. SZL's brand keeps the honest label 'Λ = Conjecture 1 — never green': the unconditional Λ-uniqueness conjecture is machine-checked open (see lutar-lean / szl-lambda-gate), so a receipt MUST NOT report Λ as 'proven'/'green'. Current per-receipt receipts express the Λ gate estate-wide via the szl-lambda-gate kernel rather than an inline numeric score, so this object is optional; when present, `score` MAY be null and `label` carries the honest status.

score.description: Numeric Λ gate score if measured, else null. Never fabricated.

label.description: Honest Λ status label. Default honest wording: 'Λ = Conjecture 1 — never green'.

note.description: Optional free-text honesty note about how the Λ label was derived.

energy.description: Measured-or-null energy accounting for the action. Joules are NEVER fabricated: when there is no live meter, `joules` is null and `label` is 'UNAVAILABLE' with an honest reason in `evidence`.

joules.description: Measured energy in joules, or null when no live meter was available (never fabricated).

label.description: Energy measurement status. Real value seen: 'UNAVAILABLE'. 'MEASURED' is used when a live NVML/meter reading was captured.

evidence.description: Honest provenance of the energy figure: which engine served, whether a meter was reachable, and why a value is/ isn't present.

chain_verified.description: Runtime self-report that the local chain was verified at emission time. The offline verifier re-checks this independently.

neuro_citations.description: Optional grounded citations attached to the governed action.

signature.description: Inline signature slot inside the decision body. In real receipts this is the placeholder 'DSSE_PLACEHOLDER'; the authoritative signature lives in the DSSE envelope (see `dsse` / `envelope`).

otel.description: OPTIONAL OpenTelemetry span link so a receipt can be joined to a trace (see vsp-otel). Spec extension; MAY be absent in current receipts.

dsseEnvelope.title: DSSE envelope (SZL profile)
dsseEnvelope.description: Dead Simple Signing Envelope carrying the signed payload. SZL adds honest extension fields (_pae_sha256, _signed_at, honesty, verify_key_url). Content is bound by the DSSE PAE: PAE = b'DSSEv1 ' + len(payloadType) + ' ' + payloadType + ' ' + len(body) + ' ' + body, and _pae_sha256 = sha256(PAE). The offline verifier recomputes this.

payloadType.description: Media type of the payload. Real value: 'application/vnd.szl.khipu+json'.

signatures.description: DSSE signatures. Each entry has a keyid and a signature; empty when signed is false.

signed.description: Whether the envelope is signed. When true, `signatures` MUST be non-empty.

_pae_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the DSSE PAE encoding of (payloadType, payload). Recomputable and checked by the verifier.

honesty.description: Honest, human-readable statement of what the signature does and does not prove.

verify_key_url.description: URL of the public key used to verify the signature.
